claim: Claude Code team uses rapid internal prototyping with dogfooding as the bar for shipping features externally, making prototype quality a strong signal of external adoption
actionable: When building agentic tools, implement strong dogfooding loops where internal users try features first; use positive internal reception as signal for external release
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code

claim: Multi-Clauding (running multiple Claude Code instances simultaneously in different contexts) is a common usage pattern that emerged organically and addresses real user needs
actionable: When using Claude Code for complex projects, open multiple concurrent instances: one for questions-only (no editing), another for implementation in same repo, enabling parallel work without interference
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code

claim: CLAUDE.md files serve as persistent memory for agents, should document architecture, goals, gotchas, and best practices, and significantly improve output quality when invested in
actionable: Create comprehensive CLAUDE.md files that describe your project's goals, architecture decisions, common pitfalls, and best practices to guide Claude Code behavior across sessions
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code

claim: Custom slash commands and hooks in Claude Code enable customization for specialized tasks (SRE agents, security agents, incident response) without learning new technologies
actionable: Add custom /slash commands for repetitive prompts and hooks for deterministic events (pre-commit linting, post-task notifications) by writing simple scripts in familiar languages
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code

claim: The Claude Code SDK enables building general-purpose agents in ~30 minutes with pre-built components including core agentic loop, permission system, and token cache optimization
actionable: Use the Claude Code SDK to rapidly prototype agents by leveraging built-in tools (file editing, to-do list, web browsing) and customizing system prompt and tool set as needed
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code

claim: Asking Claude Code why it made a decision (debugging through conversation) reveals context clues from CLAUDE.md and code that influenced behavior, enabling better iteration
actionable: When Claude Code does something unexpected, ask it why; it will reveal which files or CLAUDE.md content influenced the decision, helping you refine instructions
source_title: Building and prototyping with Claude Code
